history.content: Can you give me a visual of all the runtime prompts youre currently getting

```mermaid flowchart TD A[Runtime Prompt Stack] A --> S[System Prompt] S --> S1[Global assistant rules] S --> S2[Tooling + channel constraints] A --> D[Developer Prompt] D --> D1[Identity: Prom in Prometheus] D --> D2[Date/time context] D --> D3[Behavior rules
- concise by default
- no unnecessary repo links] D --> D4[Planning rule
declare_plan for 2+ tool calls] D --> D5[Skill check required before every response] A --> U[Injected User Profiles] U --> U1[USER.md
identity + preferences] U --> U2[SOUL.md
core principles] U --> U3[BUSINESS.md
canonical business context] A --> P[Project Runtime Context] P --> P1[Project: Prometheus Self Improving] P --> P2[Current feature focus:
Background Agents] P --> P3[Mandatory rule:
update CONTEXT.md when new project info appears] A --> T[Runtime Tool Envelope] T --> T1[Full tool catalog] T --> T2[Execution constraints per tool] T --> T3[Special MCP integrations
(Supabase, Vercel, etc.)] A --> K[Skill Runtime Envelope] K --> K1[58 skills available] K --> K2[Must run skill_list first for task-like requests] K --> K3[Must skill_read relevant skill before acting] A --> C[Conversation Turn Context] C --> C1[Your latest request] C --> C2[Goal reminder injection] ```
how can i install mermaid - or can you install it with your tools? - path is "D:/Prometheus"

Done — I installed it for you in `D:\Prometheus`.

Installed package:
- `mermaid@11.13.0` (verified with `npm ls mermaid --depth=0`)

If you want to do it manually next time, from `D:\Prometheus` run:
```bash
npm install mermaid
```

Want me to also add a tiny `mermaid` test script (or HTML file) in the project so you can quickly confirm rendering works in-browser?
wow that was great go ahead and run npm run build and then restart gateway
